page_data.title: Show and Tell
page_data.learning_objective: Speak about a familiar object using simple words and sentences.

activity.instruction: One model-and-repeat, point-and-say, listening, or role-play task for “Show and Tell”. Add one simple choice-supported speaking task.
activity.evidence: Use one exact visible 2–7 word model phrase.

teacher_prompt.facilitation: Say the model phrase slowly; accept pointing or one-word responses.
questions: What can you show or tell about show and tell?

parent_prompt.conversation: Repeat the same skill once in a familiar home routine using common materials.
parent_prompt.home_activity: Repeat the same skill once in a familiar home routine using common materials.

illustration.scene: Presentation scene plus three prompt icons
illustration.focal_point: One dominant focus for “Show and Tell”.
illustration.style: Premium BCube preschool workbook illustration; clean white base; soft pastels; thick rounded outlines.
negative_constraints: No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-NURSERY-V3-P025

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-NURSERY-V3-P025
- Version: 3.0.0
- Book: Communication Champions
- Level: Nursery (3+)
- Module: Module 3 – Friends & Communication
- Page: 25 of 41
- Exact title: Show and Tell
- Source: Communication_Champions_Gold_Individually_Rebuilt.xlsx
- Source SHA-256: caecd24a409b4f531b6018b2076d42cc9222af2f198e7c9e5835cde0bc54bfba

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait page for “Show and Tell”. Follow this deterministic package only. Produce no explanation, alternate, mockup, or additional page.

## 3. Engine order and precedence

1. Publishing Engine
2. Design Engine
3. Visual Grammar Engine
4. Educational Engine
5. Teaching Engine
6. Parent Partnership Engine
7. Illustration Engine
8. Character Engine
9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Precedence: approved page instruction > approved book/level rule > global rule. Do not resolve conflicts by guessing.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm; 3 mm bleed; minimum 10 mm safe margin; 12 mm binding margin; 300 DPI; CMYK-safe; print-ready.
- Use a 20–28 mm header with the exact title “Show and Tell” centred and the official BCube logo reserved at top left.
- Use a 10–14 mm footer with page number 25 only at bottom right.
- Never generate or redraw the logo; apply the official vector asset during final layout.

## 5. Design and Visual Grammar Engines

- Page type: activity_page. Layout anchor: Presentation scene plus three prompt icons.
- Visual/text balance: 70–80% visual and 20–30% text. Use one dominant focal point and a clear title → model → action → response eye path.
- Blank-space rule: Use at most one sentence starter. Maximum two core activities and one optional extension.
- Clean white base, soft BCube pastel accents, thick rounded outlines, large targets, strong contrast, generous spacing, and no visual clutter.

## 6. Educational Engine

- Objective: Speak about a familiar object using simple words and sentences.
- Primary activity: One model-and-repeat, point-and-say, listening, or role-play task for “Show and Tell”.
- Secondary activity: Add one simple choice-supported speaking task.
- Observable evidence: Use one exact visible 2–7 word model phrase.
- Developmental response modes: pointing, gesture, one word, short phrase, colouring, tracing, drawing, matching, or adult-supported dictation.

## 7. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Say the model phrase slowly; accept pointing or one-word responses.
- Model once, invite participation, pause for processing, scaffold through gesture/choice/model language, and affirm effort without shame.
- Teacher–student interaction must be visible, purposeful, inclusive, and connected to the exact learning objective.
- Assessment evidence: Use one exact visible 2–7 word model phrase.

## 8. Parent Partnership Engine

- Home connection: Repeat the same skill once in a familiar home routine using common materials.
- Use common household materials only; do not create a new scored task or require a device, purchase, or printing.

## 9. Illustration and Character Engines

- Composition: Gold-standard interior prompt; preserve all activity areas and exact text. Individual execution locked: 2 activities; Use at most one sentence starter.
- Show natural expressions, correct anatomy, inclusive children and families, clear turn-taking, unobstructed activity areas, and editable visible text.
- Star uses the locked bright-yellow rounded five-point identity, expressive face, blue shoes, and small blue cape; Star demonstrates or encourages without completing the child’s answer.

## 10. Approved source instruction

INDIVIDUAL PAGE EXECUTION BLUEPRINT — PAGE 25: Learning objective: Speak about a familiar object using simple words and sentences. Primary child activity: One model-and-repeat, point-and-say, listening, or role-play task for “Show and Tell”. Secondary activity: Add one simple choice-supported speaking task. Teacher/parent prompt: Say the model phrase slowly; accept pointing or one-word responses. Expected child response: Use one exact visible 2–7 word model phrase. Blank-space allocation: Use at most one sentence starter. Activity load: 2 activities. Prohibited extras: No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing. Every box, balloon, line, card, and frame must have a stated learning purpose. Do not invent additional activities or unexplained blank areas.

## 11. Negative constraints

- No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
- No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
- No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
- No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
- No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.

## 12. Gold QA gate and compiler footer

Verify page identity, geometry, bleed, safe zones, logo reservation, exact title, objective alignment, activity load, teacher interaction, parent extension, expected response, character continuity, inclusion, print suitability, prohibited extras, and unresolved variables. Any critical defect blocks release.
